name: Lady Ruqayyah
title: The Beloved Daughter
shortDescription: The 3-year-old beloved daughter of Imam Hussain (AS) who, after enduring the hardships of captivity, died in the ruins of Sham upon seeing her father's head.
fullStory:
Lady Ruqayyah (SA) was the young, three-year-old daughter of Imam Hussain (AS) present in the tragedy of Ashura. She was deeply loved by her father, and a profound emotional bond existed between them. On the day of Ashura, she witnessed the martyrdom of her loved ones and the brutal attack of Umar Sa'd's army on the tents, tasting the bitter pill of orphanhood and captivity at a tender age.

After Ashura, Ruqayyah, along with the caravan of captives, traveled the harsh and grueling route from Kufa to Sham (Damascus) on foot and on barebacked camels. Whips, desert thorns, and the cruelty of the guards battered her frail body, but missing her father was the greatest pain this child endured.

When the caravan of captives was settled in the ruins of Sham (near Yazid's palace), one night Ruqayyah saw her father in a dream. She woke up terrified, crying and asking for her father. Her crying was so heart-wrenching that Yazid woke up and, with a cruel heart, ordered the severed head of Imam Hussain (AS) to be taken to this three-year-old child.

The guards placed a tray covered with a cloth in front of Ruqayyah. The child pulled back the cloth with her small, trembling hands and suddenly faced the severed, bloody head of her father. She embraced her father's head and cried, saying: 'O father, who orphaned me at such a young age? Who severed the veins of your neck?'

Ruqayyah confided in her father's head and wept so much that she suddenly stopped moving. Lady Zaynab (SA) realized that the pure soul of this three-year-old girl had flown to the heavens in the ruins of Sham. Her holy shrine in Damascus today is a place of pilgrimage for the lovers of the Ahl al-Bayt.
quoteArabic: يا أَبَتاهُ! مَنْ ذَا الَّذي أَيْتَمَني عَلى صِغَرِ سِنّي؟
quote: O father! Who orphaned me at such a young age?
